project.name: WorldOS Engine
project.description: Rust workspace — gRPC simulation core engine for WorldOS V6 with agent behavior, universe simulation, and rule evaluation.

nodes.name: Simulation Loop
nodes.summary: Five-phase tick loop: global update, local update, diffusion/cascade, post-process, broadcast/export.

nodes.name: Core Engine
nodes.summary: Rust domain simulation core containing actors, universes, social layers, ecological dynamics, and rule evaluation.

nodes.name: Transport Layer
nodes.summary: gRPC/HTTP transport that exposes engine operations to the Laravel orchestrator and other services.

nodes.name: Rules DSL
nodes.summary: Domain-specific language and evaluator for configuring civilization and simulation rules.

nodes.name: Protobuf Contract
nodes.summary: simulation.proto defines typed service contracts shared by Rust engine and clients.

layers.name: Workspace & Build Configuration
layers.description: Cargo workspace manifests, Docker service definition, and build-time configuration for the Rust engine.

layers.name: Protobuf Contracts
layers.description: gRPC schema definitions shared by the engine transport and external clients.

layers.name: Core Domain Simulation
layers.description: worldos-core domain model and simulation primitives: agents, universes, memory, social layers, ecology, culture, and macro fields.

layers.name: Transport & Service Adapter
layers.description: worldos-grpc service layer exposing the simulation core over gRPC and HTTP, with Kafka/Redpanda integration.

layers.name: Rules DSL
layers.description: worldos-rules crate and DSL assets for parsing, evaluating, and optionally running simulation rules through WASM.

layers.name: Utility Scripts
layers.description: Small Python utilities used around the core simulation package.

tour.title: Rust Workspace Overview
tour.description: Start from the root Cargo workspace to understand the three engine crates: core simulation, transport service, and rules DSL.
tour.languageLesson: Cargo workspaces let related Rust crates share dependencies and compile together while keeping responsibilities separated.

tour.title: Core Simulation Domain
tour.description: Explore worldos-core: the agent and universe domain model, including actors, state, traits, memory, social layers, ecology, and culture.

tour.title: Five-Phase Simulation Loop
tour.description: Follow the simulation tick phases: global update, local update, diffusion/cascade, history tracking, and post-process output.

tour.title: Advanced World Dynamics
tour.description: Inspect specialized engines for cascading effects, culture, ecology, emotion fields, macro forces, and quantum-inspired observation behavior.

tour.title: Service Boundary and RPC Contracts
tour.description: Move to worldos-grpc to see how the Rust core is exposed through protobuf-generated gRPC and HTTP transports.
tour.languageLesson: Protobuf schemas define strongly typed service contracts; tonic/prost generate Rust code that implements those contracts.

tour.title: Server Runtime and Messaging
tour.description: Inspect the service executable and messaging adapter that connect the engine process to gRPC/HTTP clients and Kafka-compatible event streams.

tour.title: Rules DSL and WASM Evaluation
tour.description: Finish with the rules crate, which parses and evaluates civilization simulation rules, including optional WASM-backed execution.
